title: peltopus alveatus
side_effects: Peltopus alveatus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and stomach discomfort due to its high alkaloid content. In severe cases, excessive consumption can lead to hallucinations, irregular heartbeat, and even respiratory failure. Some individuals may also experience skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interact negatively with certain medications, resulting in adverse health consequences. If not handled correctly, the plant's toxic compounds can cause long-term damage to organs such as the liver and kidneys.
